title: Visa Company Overview
text: Visa is a global payments technology company that facilitates secure, reliable, and efficient commerce and money movement among consumers, sellers, financial institutions, businesses, strategic partners, and governments. Its core network provides transaction processing services, primarily authorization, clearing, and settlement, across more than 200 countries and territories. Visa is not a bank and generally does not issue cards, extend credit, or set rates and fees for Visa account holders. Revenue is mainly connected to service revenue from payments volume, data processing revenue from processed transactions, international transaction revenue from cross-border activity, and other value-added services, reduced by client incentives. For investment analysis, Visa is important because it benefits from the global shift from cash to digital payments, ecommerce growth, travel and cross-border spending, tokenization, fraud tools, Visa Direct, commercial payments, and value-added services. Key risks include regulation of payment fees and routing, litigation, macro weakness in consumer spending, intense competition from Mastercard and fintechs, cybersecurity, and disruption from new payment technologies.
